channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Everyone Wants to Tax A.I. The Big Disagreement: How?
item.description: Bernie Sanders, President Trump and even A.I. companies say they would like the public to share the wealth. But their solutions are very different.
item.dc:creator: Peter Coy

item.title: Wages Are Falling. Wealth Is Surging. No Wonder Americans Are Unhappy.
item.description: As Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire, workers are facing higher prices and fears of A.I.-driven job losses.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Times Square on Friday, when Elon Musk’s SpaceX went public on the Nasdaq exchange.

item.title: Mega I.P.O. Frenzy Could Be a Harbinger of a Stock Bubble
item.description: Rampant enthusiasm is buoying tech shares to levels that defy gravity. Invest with caution, our columnist says.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer

item.title: Despite US Help, Little Oil Has Gone Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said more than 200 commercial vessels had safely traveled through the strait. That’s still far fewer than before the start of the war.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Global stockpiles of oil continue to decline as vessels remain stuck, unable to transit the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: At the Canadian Screen Awards, the Industry Steps Outside Hollywood’s Shadow
item.description: No longer content with being just “Hollywood North,” the Canadian screen industry is asserting itself and telling its own stories.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Cole Burston
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actor Robbie Graham-Kunz speaking with fans outside the 14th Canadian Screen Awards in Toronto.

item.title: Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire. Who Was the First Billionaire?
item.description: John D. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became America’s first billionaire in 1916. Like Elon Musk’s ascension, the milestone made headlines.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:description: John D. Rockefeller Sr., in Lakewood, N.J., in 1933. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became a billionaire in 1916.

item.title: Justice Dept. Clears Way for Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: The $111 billion deal would unite two major movie studios and put CNN under the same roof as CBS News.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, David McCabe and Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery would include the Warner Bros. movie studio, above, in Burbank, Calif.

item.title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
item.description: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Kenneth Chang and Kirsten Grind
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: A SpaceX ad on the Nasdaq MarketSite tower in Times Square. The company, which Elon Musk founded in 2002, began trading on the stock market on Friday.

item.title: The steady hand at SpaceX is not Elon Musk’s.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: Gwynne Shotwell, SpaceX’s president and chief operating officer, in Miami Beach last year during one of her infrequent public appearances. “I love working for Elon,” she has said.

item.title: SpaceX is structured in a way that appears to benefit Musk at the expense of other shareholders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: Central to Elon Musk’s power over SpaceX is his control of “super voting” shares.

item.title: Elon Musk laid out 602 goals. We counted how many he hit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grind, Jack Ewing, Aaron Krolik and Lily Boyce

item.title: Elon Musk’s Big Bet for SpaceX
item.description: Elon Musk is taking SpaceX public and keeping almost all the power. Ryan Mac, a technology reporter for The Times, breaks down why the company’s record I.P.O. is both a big bet on Musk’s vision and a risk for investors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Sam Rosenthal

item.title: If You Have a 401(k), How Much SpaceX Stock Will You Own?
item.description: If you have index funds — such as in a 401(k) — they may soon include shares of the mega company. Here’s a rough calculation.
item.dc:creator: C.J. Robinson

item.title: Musk posted about politics, race and the culture wars, even as he took SpaceX public.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger
item.media:description: Elon Musk at the Kennedy Center in Washington, in 2025.

item.title: SpaceX is about to be in your 401(k) whether you like it or not.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and Maureen Farrell
item.media:description: SpaceX initial public offering signage is displayed at the Bank of America building in New York.

item.title: About 20 New Billionaires Could Be Minted by 3 Mega-I.P.O.s
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI could join SpaceX with their own initial public offerings, setting many employees up to become millionaires or billionaires.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dario Amodei, the co-founder and chief executive of Anthropic, in December.

item.title: Blake Lively Awarded Legal Fees in Ruling After Justin Baldoni Settlement
item.description: The size of the award remains to be determined. It only involves the legal fees associated with part of her court battle with Mr. Baldoni, with whom she made a film.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: A judge on Friday awarded Blake Lively some legal fees in her court battle with Justin Baldoni. The amount was not set in the ruling.

item.title: Elon Musk Becomes World’s First Trillionaire as SpaceX Stock Begins Trading
item.description: With SpaceX shares soaring 20 percent on their first day of trading, the world’s richest person crossed another milestone — one with 13 digits.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Ben Casselman
item.media:description: Elon Musk, the chief executive of SpaceX, in 2022 at the company’s facility in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX Stock Rises 11% in Largest IPO Ever
item.description: The blockbuster stock market debut of Elon Musk’s rocket company paves the way for a wave of enormous offerings that are coming from OpenAI and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Traders at the New York Stock Exchange watching news about SpaceX’s initial public offering on the Nasdaq exchange on Friday.

item.title: Is SpaceX worth $1.77 trillion? It’s a pie in the sky, some investors say.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:description: SpaceX set an initial public offering price of $135 a share, valuing it at $1.77 trillion. Some investors are skeptical that the valuation is justified.

item.title: The World Cup Could Be the Biggest Sports Gambling Event Ever
item.description: At a time of sluggish growth, betting sites like FanDuel and DraftKings are counting on the global soccer tournament to boost online sports gambling.
item.dc:creator: Joe Drape

item.title: When will SpaceX start trading?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: SpaceX advertisements in Times Square on Friday morning, ahead of its stock’s debut trading.

item.title: Americans Aren’t Money Savvy, and They’re Only Getting Worse
item.description: Researchers say people with poor understanding of basic finances often make bad decisions on money matters.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch

item.title: The Consequences of SpaceX’s Trillion-Dollar I.P.O.
item.description: SpaceX’s trading debut is expected to smash records. Wall Street is watching to see what else it disrupts.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Christine Zhang
item.media:description: SpaceX’s market debut today is expected to be a watershed moment for Wall Street.

item.title: Risk Strait of Hormuz or Wait? Shipping Companies Face a Costly Dilemma.
item.description: With more than 500 ships still stranded in the Persian Gulf, pressure on the shipowners and sailors is growing by the day.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Supplies of fresh food and water have been running low for the roughly 11,000 sailors stuck on hundreds of ships anchored in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: How SpaceX’s IPO Compares With Saudi Aramco, Uber and Others
item.description: The previous record was set over six years ago. But SpaceX, whose stock began trading on Friday, is on track to raise tens of billions of dollars more.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Gu

item.title: The World Is Draining Oil Reserves, Raising Pressure for a Peace Deal
item.description: The amount of oil and fuel stored by businesses and governments has fallen sharply since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Lazaro Gamio
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Guide to Chicago’s South Side, Home of the New Obama Center
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center, opening on June 19, is poised to attract visitors to an often-overlooked area. Here’s what you’ll find in Hyde Park and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac and Akilah Townsend
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:description: The $850-million Obama Presidential Center will introduce visitors to an area they have largely overlooked in favor of downtown attractions. Above, the sculpture of the Obamas outside the center was created by StudioEIS, a Brooklyn-based design studio.

item.title: Oil Falls, Stocks Rise as Trump Signals Iran Peace Deal Takes Shape
item.description: Oil prices retreated and stocks rallied after President Trump called off plans for another day of strikes on Iran, saying that a peace deal could be within reach.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: SpaceX IPO: How Our Reporters Assess the Sky-High Valuation and Potential Economic Impact
item.description: Two Times tech reporters and their editor talk about assessing the sky-high valuation of the rocket and satellite maker, the potential economic impact of its public debut and the role of the world’s richest man in it all.
item.dc:creator: Megan DiTrolio
item.media:description: Elon Musk’s rocket maker SpaceX set a price of $135 a share, which would value it at $1.77 trillion.

item.title: Cleve Moler, Who Unlocked the Power of Computing for Millions, Dies at 86
item.description: He built interfaces that allowed engineers, scientists and everyday people to solve difficult problems without having to write the underlying code.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: SpaceX Finalizes IPO Price at $135 a Share in World’s Largest Public Offering
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said it would sell more than 555 million shares at $135 each in its blockbuster initial public offering, which is set to begin trading on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: SpaceX will begin trading publicly on Friday under the ticker symbol SPCX.

item.title: Absent From the SpaceX and OpenAI I.P.O.s? Chinese Investors.
item.description: SpaceX will not raise money from investors in China and Hong Kong. Others firms, like OpenAI, may follow suit.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson, Maureen Farrell and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: A Starship test flight at the SpaceX’s Starbase in Texas last month. Investors in China are expected to be excluded from the SpaceX initial public offering.

item.title: Skeptics Question Whether SpaceX Is Worth $1.77 Trillion
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company is spending big and losing money. That has raised questions about whether it can justify its valuation for its blockbuster initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:description: SpaceX has set an initial public offering price of $135 a share, which would value it at $1.77 trillion. Some investors are skeptical that the valuation is justified.

item.title: The SpaceX Shareholder at the Helm of a Multibillion-Dollar Payout
item.description: Justin Fishner-Wolfson, long a minor figure in Elon Musk’s orbit, is at the helm of a multibillion-dollar payout.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland
item.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justin Fishner-Wolfson’s firm now owns more than 1 percent of SpaceX, worth roughly $20 billion at the company’s expected $1.77 trillion valuation at its listing price.

item.title: Boston Symphony in Crisis Over Dismissal of Andris Nelsons
item.description: Chad Smith, the orchestra’s president, admitted missteps in terminating Andris Nelsons’s contract but stood by the decision and won’t step down.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney and Robin Pogrebin

item.title: The Joy of Missing Out on SpaceX’s I.P.O.
item.description: You’re not important or lucky enough to have access to shares at the offering price, and that’s OK. Here’s why.
item.dc:creator: Ron Lieber

item.title: Can’t Pay Medical Bills? Trump Administration Suggests Getting a Loan
item.description: One-third of Americans shoulder health care debt. Insurers are being asked to consider lending money to Obamacare consumers who can’t afford higher deductibles.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson
item.category: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (2010)
item.media:credit: Cig Harvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kathleen Capetta’s family of five is paying an additional $750 a month this year for its Obamacare plan premium. She was already paying off debt from cancer treatment.

item.title: World Bank Warns Iran War Is Slowing Global Growth
item.description: The world economy is weakening as energy prices fuel a new bout of inflation.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: An explosion after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes hit oil storage depots in Tehran. The war has tangled supply chains and caused oil, gas and fertilizer prices to gyrate for months.

item.title: Can SpaceX Defy A.I. Gravity?
item.description: Elon Musk’s rockets-and-artificial-intelligence company is going public as questions grow over the incredible spending spree taking place in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:description: SpaceX’s launchpad for its Starship rocket in March. Investors may be more focused on the company’s artificial intelligence business.

item.title: How Tesla’s Stock Listing in 2010 Enabled SpaceX’s I.P.O.
item.description: Investors who bought the automaker’s shares in its initial public offering became rich. As a result, many have a deep faith in Elon Musk, the chief executive of Tesla and SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:description: During Tesla’s initial public offering in 2010, Elon Musk, the company’s chief executive, said, “The smartest money in the world is betting on Tesla.”

item.title: In the Protein Era, Even He-Man Is Hawking Supplements
item.description: To promote “Masters of the Universe,” Mattel, the toymaker behind the movie, tapped into one of the hottest trends in health and wellness.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt
item.media:credit: Brendan George Ko for The New York Times
item.media:description: Perfect Sports has introduced an exclusive line of nutritional products tied to “Masters of the Universe.”

item.title: How to File a Travel Insurance Claim
item.description: The process involves attention to detail and, above all, documentation. Here’s what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac

item.title: Why It’s Nearly Impossible to Build a Robot Without China
item.description: Building on the country’s electric vehicle industry, Chinese companies are making robot parts at a scale and price point others can’t match.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
